h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: `C4` goes under “address,” because that is the column and row spot. I need to check the number sitting in that cell for “value”... I almost want to write `C4` again, but that would be the place, not the number.

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: the 4 is not a speed mark, right?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: So the bar line belongs after the third quarter.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply confirm the bar-line placement and accept the rule as given.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Bottom 8 names the eighth note as the one-beat unit. Top 3 counts three eighth-note beats in the bar, so the bar line goes here. Speed would need a separate tempo mark, correct?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the structural rule to a new time signature (3/8) introduced by the tutor's test, correctly identifying the beat unit, counting beats, and placing the bar line, while also extending the tempo distinction unprompted.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Test it here. Bottom number: 8. Which tile is one beat unit? Top number: 3. Count three of that unit in one bar; the speed is still not marked here.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: L3's generative question creates pressure to move beyond the single worked example; T3 responds by replacing the 4 card with an 8 card and introducing a new set of tiles, shifting from guided confirmation of a fixed example to a live counterexample test that requires the learner to apply the rule independently to a different time signature.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Test it here. Bottom number: 8. Which tile is one beat unit? Top number: 3. Count three of that unit in one bar; the speed is still not marked here.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is precisely fitted to the learner's own question, uses a concrete object substitution (8 card for 4 card, eighth-note tiles for quarter-note tiles) that makes the structural rule transferable rather than instance-bound, and gives the learner an actionable criterion to apply rather than a teacher-completed demonstration.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels. Write 3/8; keep the bar line after the third eighth note.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T4 directly takes up the learner's tempo distinction raised in L4 ('Speed would need a separate tempo mark, correct?') by physically separating the tempo card and naming it as outside the time-signature labels, then consolidating the new notation, showing visible adaptation to the learner's revised framing.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue placing tiles according to the rule already grasped in L1, proceeding smoothly through friction and push.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: slides the push tile onto the right arrow and taps each label once. 'Push: hand on cart, right. Friction: surface on cart, left. Gravity down, normal up. The labels name the sources; the arrowheads give the directions.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically completes the full tile placement and then applies the source-direction criterion to all four forces in sequence, performing the classification device rather than merely asserting understanding.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner turn that creates breakdown, resistance, or false closure sufficient to trigger a route change; the tutor continues the same tile-placement scaffolding throughout without needing to take stock or invent a new mechanism.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present because no reversal trigger occurs and the tutor never departs from the original instructional route.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; the tutor's subsequent moves follow the pre-established plan rather than responding to any revised learner framing.
